column13: Very difficult to communicate with and always hard to get timely responses from.  Very rarely in the hospital.  When responding to questions about procedures she often becomes angry and often doesn't want to be bothered with important issues involving patient care.  She is not approachable and often does not take residents concerns about patient care seriously.
Dr. Baz, the medical director of blood bank at AUB is a very sweet and excellent doctor. She takes care of patients and is involved with other physicians to provide.
Although Dr. Abadjian doesn't work with us anymore, but I always admired his calmness and professionalism with the staff.
NOT APPLICABLE FOR ME, I DIDN'T WORK DIRECTLY WITH DR.ABADJIAN.
I feel honored and privileged to be part of the team under the jurisdiction of Dr George Araj for he is a great mentor and leader.

Dr araj is a father figure in the department, always excited and ready to help the residents in their learning and in their personal lives. He is very generous, his door is open to everyone and he is respected and loved by all the technicians and residents. He loves to work on and involve residents in new research projects, and is always ready to teach residents in lab students.
Sometimes he uses Arabic too much when speaking in the presence of me and he knows I need him to speak English so I can learn properly.

Highly professional,caring for patients, staff , and residents,
trustworthy, father figure of the department.
Dr. Araj is the an excellent medical director of microbiology lab at AUB.

Always dedicated to providing outstanding services to patient care and customers. Also, maintain keenness to updated knowledge in education and fostering research while mentoring Technical Staff, Students, Residents and Fellows in a spirit of collaboration and teamwork including Colleagues.
Needless to mention that my tolerance to aberrant behavior and issues is zero.

Dr. Jessica always seem to have a positive attitude towards everyone. She's very respectful, energetic, and always seem to care about improving her skills. Although she is relatively new in our Pathology department, yet everyone seem to like her and admire her sweet heart.
Dr. Nada is very professional, dedicated to her job. proud to be working under her supervision
Dr. Nada Assaf is professional, respectful, patient caring and her attitude is always positive.
Dr Assaf is very professional, always ready to help and teach residents, very motivated in her section and do a lot of research. She is loved and respected by all technicians and residents.
Very professional and respectful at all times.
– Excellent attending physician, professional, highly involved in academic teaching and research activities.
Dr. Assaf is professional on all levels.

Dr. Shabb is such a role model for being the perfect combination between professionalism and pleasurable. 
Whenever she enters the Lab, she always says Hello to all the staff, nicely enough even if the staff is far in distance. 
She is easy to talk to, very understanding, and treats the staff with friendliness and respect.
Patient safety and quality of result is her first and primary concern.

I always go out of my way to help in any way possible when it comes to patient care with all the limited resources we have especially in terms of staffing.

Always listen to the concerns of physicians and respond to their inquiries with full evidence the soonest possible.

All what I do is to improve patient safety with a professional and collegian atmosphere.
Lovely and one of the most dedicated persons

Dr Daher is very professional and dedicated to quality control in the laboratory. She is always eager to teach the residents wether through daily signout or lectures. She is a role model to the technicians and residents.

Dr. Daher is one of the best. She is the director of the chemistry part which runs always on time, very smoothly, and with exceptional quality. I learned a lot from Dr. Daher during the chemistry rotation. Looking forward to my second.
Highly professional. He is a role–model.
Awarded the GOLD HUMANISM HONOR SOCIETY award in MAY 2023.

I have been working with Dr. Mahfouz for almost 20 years and have not met a professional, dedicated, outstanding, and passionate person about his job as he is.
I am privileged and honoured to have such a remarkable boss.

Dr. Mahfouz has shown great commitment to the growth of the department and to the institution. "He leads by example" and He is always available to support his team and his costumers. Dr. Mahfouz is a valuable asset to this organisation.

Dr Mahfouz is very professional, always ready to help the residents on all levels. He listens to residents concerns and take them into consideration always. He is very dedicated to his work and he is loved by everyone in the department.

Dr. Mahfouz, the medical director of molecular laboratory at AUB is exceptional. He is an amazing teacher as well as an excellent advisor to all of his residents. Rotating in molecular biology, I learned a lot. He is dedicated to providing excellent and meticulous patient care. Add to that, the didactic lectures provided by Dr. Mahfouz are educational and always updating about the latest developments in the field.

Although Dr. Sinno is a relatively new attending doctor, yet she shows great traits. I know Dr. Sinno since she was a resident and she always showed great respect to everyone she worked with. 
A highlight about Dr. Sinno is the fact that she cares a lot about her work down to the smallest details. For instance, if we ever call her to her office, and fail to reach her, she would call again as soon as she sees a call from the Lab. Another example, is that she would come in person to the lab, to ask of us a special request rather than doing it over the phone, ofcourse, always with a smile and thank you phrase.
I consider Dr. Zaatari a role model in professionalism.

Empathy, flexibility, and “problem–solving” skills are 3 essential skills that make him a very good lab director. Dr. Zaher manages unusual, rare, or complex disorders. He leads by example and works on identifying issues and resolving them as quickly as possible. He uses creative problem solving which ultimately leads to promoting a good and positive environment in the lab while giving positive feedback when it's needed. he is a great listener and cares for his team and the team"s growth.

Sometimes, Dr. Chakhachiro treats the staff with disrespect, and undervalue their efforts. In some occasions he uses abusive and rude intimidating language which creates an uncomfortable work environment, and discourages the staff to raise their concerns or inquiries, for fear of retaliation.

On the behavior side, Dr. Chakhachiro, in certain occasions, addresses the staff with disrespect.
In the technical side, sometimes he does not respond to their inquiries timely which ends up by delaying patients' results.
